The book of beginnings, detailing creation, the fall of man, the promise of redemption, and God's covenant with the patriarchs: Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and Joseph.
The record of Israel's deliverance from Egypt under Moses, the covenant and Ten Commandments given at Mount Sinai, and the construction of the Tabernacle where God dwelt with His people.
A manual of holiness and worship for Israel, outlining laws regarding sacrifices, priesthood, ritual purity, and the Day of Atonement, highlighting the holiness of God.
The history of Israel's journey through the wilderness, their rebellion and subsequent 40-year wandering, and the preparation of the new generation to enter the Promised Land.
Moses' final sermons to Israel before entering the Promised Land, reviewing the Law, reminding them of God's faithfulness, and urging them to choose life through obedience.
The record of Israel's conquest and division of the Promised Land under the leadership of Joshua, demonstrating God's faithfulness to His covenant promises.
The dark history of Israel's cycles of rebellion, oppression, repentance, and deliverance through God-raised leaders (judges), highlighting the need for a righteous king.
A beautiful story of loyalty, redemption, and grace, where Ruth, a Moabite widow, is brought into the line of David and Christ through her kinsman-redeemer, Boaz.
The transition of Israel from judges to a monarchy, recounting the lives of Samuel the prophet, Saul the first king, and the rise of David as God's chosen king.
The reign of King David over Israel, detailing his military victories, spiritual devotion, moral failures, and the everlasting covenant God established with his lineage.
The height of Israel's glory under Solomon, the construction of the Temple, and the tragic division of the nation into the Northern (Israel) and Southern (Judah) kingdoms.
The decline and fall of both kingdoms, Israel to Assyria and Judah to Babylon, because of their persistent idolatry, featuring the prophets Elijah and Elisha.
A priestly perspective on Israel's history, focusing on the genealogy of the tribes, the reign of King David, and the preparations for building the Temple.
The history of the Southern Kingdom of Judah from Solomon's glory to the Babylonian exile, highlighting the spiritual reforms of its few righteous kings.
The return of the Jewish exiles from Babylon to Jerusalem under Cyrus's decree, the rebuilding of the Temple, and the spiritual reformation led by Ezra the scribe.
The rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls under the leadership of Nehemiah, and the joint efforts with Ezra to restore the law, covenant, and worship among the returned exiles.
A story of God's silent providential care, where a young Jewish queen, Esther, risks her life to save her people from annihilation in the Persian empire.
A profound exploration of suffering, faith, and the sovereignty of God, as Job loses everything yet remains faithful, learning to trust God's infinite wisdom.
A collection of 150 prayers, praises, and songs of worship, expressing the full range of human emotion and pointing prophetically to the Messiah, Jesus Christ.
A treasury of divine wisdom, practical instruction, and moral guidance for daily living, contrasting the way of wisdom with the way of folly.
A philosophical reflection on the meaning of life, declaring that earthly pursuits are vanity apart from fearing God and keeping His commandments.
An allegorical and literal celebration of love, marriage, and marital intimacy, representing God's deep love for His people.
A major prophetic book proclaiming warnings of judgment, warnings to trust in God, and glorious promises of the coming Messiah, the Suffering Servant who would redeem His people.
The warnings of the "weeping prophet" to Judah before the Babylonian captivity, promising restoration and a future New Covenant written on their hearts.
A collection of five tragic poems mourning the destruction of Jerusalem and the Temple by Babylon, while expressing hope in God's great faithfulness and mercies.
Visions and prophecies given to Ezekiel during the exile in Babylon, showing God's departed glory, judgment on the nations, and the promise of a restored Temple and new heart.
The faith of Daniel and his friends in exile, including miraculous deliverances and apocalyptic prophecies of earthly empires and the establishment of God's eternal Kingdom.
The prophet Hosea's marriage to an unfaithful wife, serving as a powerful visual aid of God's grieving love and redeeming faithfulness toward unfaithful Israel.
A warning of the coming "Day of the LORD" symbolized by a devastating locust plague, calling for repentance and promising the future outpouring of God's Spirit.
A message of social justice and divine judgment against Israel's wealthy class for exploiting the poor and practicing hypocritical worship.
A brief prophecy declaring judgment on Edom for pride and their betrayal of Judah during the Babylonian invasion, promising ultimate restoration for Mount Zion.
The story of a rebellious prophet sent to Nineveh, demonstrating God's wide mercy for the Gentiles and the sign of Jonah prefiguring Christ's resurrection.
Prophecies of judgment against social oppression and corrupt leadership, alongside promises of mercy and the specific prophecy of the Messiah's birth in Bethlehem.
A prophetic oracle declaring the destruction of the brutal city of Nineveh, vindicating God's justice and bringing comfort to Judah.
A dialogue between the prophet and God regarding why evil prospered, declaring that the just shall live by faith and trusting in God's sovereignty.
Warnings of the sweeping judgment of the Day of the LORD on Judah and surrounding nations, concluding with a promise of restoration and singing over a remnant.
Post-exilic messages encouraging the returned remnant to put God first and complete the rebuilding of the second Temple, promising future glory.
Apocalyptic visions and prophecies encouraging the Temple rebuild, filled with messianic predictions of Christ's entry on a donkey, betrayal, and second coming.
The final Old Testament book, rebuking Israel's spiritual apathy and corrupt priesthood, promising the coming Messenger of the Covenant and the Sun of Righteousness.
The Gospel written primarily to Jews, presenting Jesus as the Messiah, the King of kings, and the fulfillment of Old Testament prophecy, featuring the Sermon on the Mount.
The fast-paced Gospel presenting Jesus as the Suffering Servant of God, focusing on His actions, miracles, and ultimate sacrifice for mankind.
A detailed, historical Gospel presenting Jesus as the compassionate Son of Man who came to seek and to save the lost, emphasizing His humanity, parables, and prayers.
The theological Gospel presenting Jesus as the eternal Word of God made flesh, focusing on His divinity, the "I Am" statements, and the gift of eternal life through faith.
The history of the early Church after Christ's ascension, detailing the outpouring of the Holy Spirit, the spread of the Gospel, and the missionary journeys of Peter and Paul.
Paul's systematic theological explanation of the Gospel, detailing the sinfulness of man, justification by faith through Christ, sanctification, and Christian living.
Paul's letter addressing conflicts, divisions, and moral issues in the church at Corinth, providing instructions on love, spiritual gifts, and the resurrection.
Paul's defense of his apostolic authority and ministry, expressing his deep love for the Corinthian church and outlining the ministry of reconciliation.
A defense of the Gospel of grace against legalists, declaring that justification is by faith in Christ alone, apart from the works of the Law.
A letter detailing the believer's spiritual blessings in Christ, the unity of Jews and Gentiles in the Church, and practical instructions for walking in love and putting on the armor of God.
A warm letter written from prison, encouraging joy in all circumstances, highlighting Christ's humility, and urging believers to press toward the goal.
A letter asserting the absolute supremacy and sufficiency of Jesus Christ over all creation and philosophy, warning against false teachings.
Paul's encouragement to a young church, praising their faith and love, and comforting them with the promise of Christ's second coming.
Clarifications regarding the Day of the Lord and the second coming, urging believers to stand firm and work quietly while waiting.
Practical guidance and instructions from Paul to his young disciple Timothy on church leadership, sound doctrine, worship, and qualifications for elders and deacons.
Paul's final, moving letter before his martyrdom, urging Timothy to remain faithful, preach the Word, and endure suffering for the Gospel.
Paul's instruction to Titus on organizing the churches in Crete, appointing godly leaders, teaching sound doctrine, and practicing good works.
A personal plea from Paul to a Christian slave-owner to forgive and receive his runaway slave, Onesimus, as a beloved brother in Christ.
A sermon demonstrating that Jesus Christ is superior to angels, Moses, the Levitical priesthood, and the Old Covenant, calling believers to hold fast to their faith.
A practical exhortation on living out one's faith, demonstrating that true saving faith produces good works, patience in trials, and control of the tongue.
Encouragement and hope for suffering Christians, urging them to live holy lives, submit to authority, and look to Christ as their example.
Warnings against false teachers and mockers who deny Christ's return, urging believers to grow in grace and the knowledge of Jesus.
A letter of assurance and fellowship, declaring that God is light and love, and providing tests of true fellowship: obedience, love, and sound doctrine.
A brief warning to a local church to walk in truth and love, and to reject false teachers who deny that Jesus came in the flesh.
A brief letter commending Gaius for his hospitality and truth, while warning against the proud leadership of Diotrephes.
A passionate exhortation to contend earnestly for the faith against ungodly men who have crept into the church, concluding with a magnificent doxology.
The apocalyptic vision of Jesus Christ given to John, detailing the tribulation, the final defeat of Satan and evil, and the glorious creation of the New Heaven and New Earth.
